questions.q: In Hindu tradition, what kind of being is Raktabija?
questions.explanation: Raktabija is an asura, a demon described in the Devi Mahatmya.

questions.q: What is the special boon associated with the demon Raktabija?
options: He could fly through the heavens
He could turn invisible at will
Every drop of his blood that touched the ground became a new clone of him
questions.explanation: Raktabija's boon caused a new duplicate of him to spring up wherever a drop of his blood fell on the ground.

questions.q: Which goddess ultimately drank Raktabija's blood to defeat him?
questions.explanation: The fierce goddess Kali drank Raktabija's blood before it could touch the ground, preventing him from multiplying.

questions.q: In which sacred text does the story of Raktabija appear?
questions.explanation: The episode of Raktabija is narrated in the Devi Mahatmya, a portion of the Markandeya Purana.

questions.q: How did Kali prevent new copies of Raktabija from forming?
options: By extending her tongue to drink each drop of blood before it fell
By binding him with a rope
questions.explanation: Kali extended her tongue and drank every drop of Raktabija's blood before it could reach the ground.

questions.q: The name 'Raktabija' is most closely connected to which element of the story?
options: His blood, from which clones arose
questions.explanation: The name Raktabija means 'blood-seed,' referring to the boon by which his blood produced new demons.

questions.q: In the Devi Mahatmya, Raktabija fights as a champion in the army of which demon kings?
questions.explanation: In the Devi Mahatmya, Raktabija is a powerful demon dispatched into battle by the brothers Shumbha and Nishumbha.

questions.q: In the Devi Mahatmya, against whom does Raktabija wage war?
questions.explanation: Raktabija battles the Goddess (Devi) and her emanations during the war against the asuras.

questions.q: What made Raktabija so difficult for the Goddess's forces to kill in battle?
options: He could not be wounded by any weapon
Each wound spilled blood that created more copies of him
He was protected by an unbreakable shield
questions.explanation: Wounding Raktabija only multiplied him, because each fallen drop of blood produced a new demon.

questions.q: The Devi Mahatmya, which contains the Raktabija episode, is part of which larger work?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya is embedded within the Markandeya Purana.

questions.q: In the Devi Mahatmya, the great Goddess who leads the battle against Raktabija is most often called by which name?
questions.explanation: The Goddess is invoked as Durga, also named Chandika and Ambika, in the Devi Mahatmya.

questions.q: According to the Devi Mahatmya, from whom did Kali emerge to confront Raktabija?
options: From the brow (forehead) of the wrathful Goddess
questions.explanation: Kali sprang forth from the furrowed brow of the angry Goddess Ambika in the Devi Mahatmya.

questions.q: In the Devi Mahatmya, Kali who drinks Raktabija's blood is also addressed by which fearsome name?
questions.explanation: Kali is called Chamunda, a fierce form invoked during the battle in the Devi Mahatmya.

questions.q: What role do the Matrikas (mother goddesses) play in the Raktabija episode of the Devi Mahatmya?
options: They join the Goddess in striking Raktabija while Kali drinks his blood
questions.explanation: The Matrikas assail Raktabija with their weapons while Kali consumes the spilled blood, denying him new clones.

questions.q: In the Devi Mahatmya, the two demon chiefs whose campaign Raktabija's battle is part of are best known as which pair?
questions.explanation: Raktabija fights in the war against the Goddess on behalf of the demon brothers Shumbha and Nishumbha.

questions.q: Why was simply striking Raktabija with weapons counterproductive for the Goddess's forces?
options: The weapons shattered on his skin
Every drop of his blood that fell gave rise to a fresh demon
He absorbed the weapons into his body
He grew larger with each blow
questions.explanation: Striking him drew blood, and each fallen drop produced another Raktabija, multiplying the enemy.

questions.q: In the iconography of the Raktabija episode, Kali is typically shown with which striking feature used against the demon?
questions.explanation: Kali is depicted with a great extended tongue, the means by which she laps up Raktabija's blood.

questions.q: Within the Devi Mahatmya, the Raktabija episode falls in the section devoted chiefly to which deity?
questions.explanation: The entire Devi Mahatmya glorifies the Devi, the supreme Goddess who vanquishes Raktabija.

questions.q: How did the Goddess and Kali finally bring about Raktabija's death after his blood was contained?
options: The Goddess struck him down once his blood no longer produced clones
They drowned him in the sea
questions.explanation: Once Kali drank his blood so no new demons arose, the Goddess slew the now-isolated Raktabija.

questions.q: In the Devi Mahatmya, what happened to the clones of Raktabija that had already formed during the fight?
options: They were devoured and destroyed by Kali along with the blood
questions.explanation: Kali consumed the multiplied Raktabijas together with the blood, clearing them from the field.

questions.q: The Devi Mahatmya is traditionally recited during which major festival devoted to the Goddess?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya, including the Raktabija episode, is recited during Navaratri and Durga Puja.

questions.q: Which class of demons does Raktabija belong to in the Devi Mahatmya's account?
questions.explanation: Raktabija is counted among the asuras who oppose the gods and the Goddess.

questions.q: The Sanskrit name 'Raktabija' is a compound; what does the element 'bija' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Rakta' means blood and 'bija' means seed, so Raktabija is the demon whose blood acts as seed for new demons.

questions.q: In which canto/episode-sequence of the Devi Mahatmya does the slaying of Raktabija occur?
options: In the first episode, with the slaying of Madhu and Kaitabha
In the later episode of the war with Shumbha and Nishumbha
Before the creation of the cosmos
In a frame story told to a merchant alone
questions.explanation: Raktabija is slain during the final cycle of the Devi Mahatmya, the war against Shumbha and Nishumbha.

questions.q: The Matrikas who fight alongside the Goddess against Raktabija are described as the energies (shaktis) of which beings?
options: Of various great gods such as Brahma, Vishnu, and Shiva
Of the ocean and the rivers
questions.explanation: The Matrikas are the shaktis of major gods like Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva, and others, emanated to aid the Goddess.

questions.q: Which Matrika, the shakti of Vishnu, is named among those who battle Raktabija in the Devi Mahatmya?
questions.explanation: Vaishnavi, the shakti of Vishnu, is one of the Matrikas who strike Raktabija while Kali drinks his blood.

questions.q: In the Devi Mahatmya, after Kali drinks the blood, by what additional means is Raktabija weakened before being slain?
options: The Goddess's weapons keep wounding him while no new blood reaches the ground
He is starved for a year
He is tricked into a riddle contest
He is bound by a serpent
questions.explanation: The Goddess and Matrikas continue to strike him, draining his strength while Kali catches every drop of blood.

questions.q: The fierce form Chamunda, associated with drinking Raktabija's blood, derives her name in the Devi Mahatmya from slaying which two demons?
questions.explanation: Kali is called Chamunda because she slew the demon generals Chanda and Munda, and in this form she drinks Raktabija's blood.

questions.q: What broader theological idea is commonly drawn from the Raktabija episode in the Devi Mahatmya?
options: That the gods are stronger than the Goddess
That demons can never be defeated
That desire or ego, like spilled blood, multiplies unless wholly consumed
questions.explanation: Interpreters often read Raktabija's multiplying blood as a symbol of proliferating desires or ego that must be entirely consumed.

questions.q: In the Devi Mahatmya, what does the Goddess instruct or expect Kali (Chamunda) to do as the Matrikas attack Raktabija?
options: To retreat and guard the heavens
To roam the battlefield drinking the falling blood and devouring the clones
questions.explanation: Kali is to consume every drop of blood and the demons born from it, so that Raktabija cannot regenerate.
